previous_verdict.convergent_condition: real-subprocess timeout test required; mocks insufficient

votes.top_risk: Wall-clock ceiling (3.0s) is generous enough that mild serialization would still pass; 1.01s actual is strong but lacks an explicit lower-bound proof.
votes.what_would_change_mind: Evidence that subprocess.run is intercepted by a fixture or conftest not visible in the excerpts.

votes.top_risk: Over-interpreting 1.01s as a complete concurrency proof — one bounded integration scenario, not a broader stress test under varied scheduler load.
votes.what_would_change_mind: Evidence that run_council can serialize under real subprocess execution in another supported path, or the integration test bypasses the real production timeout mechanism.

votes.top_risk: CI flakiness — real-subprocess tests susceptible to timing jitter under CPU steal; 3.0s ceiling is generous but could produce false negatives on resource-constrained runners.
votes.what_would_change_mind: Evidence of mock-leakage or serial execution — if daemon.council uses a hidden internal timer rather than true threading with subprocess.run.

votes.top_risk: Wall-clock ceiling is generous; test lacks an explicit lower-bound assertion to catch a no-op or short-circuit regression where the hang CLI never actually launches.
votes.what_would_change_mind: Evidence that subprocess.run is intercepted by a fixture/conftest; CI run showing wall-clock >= ceiling; _CLI_ARGV patching shown to bypass subprocess.run code path.

votes.top_risk: Wall-clock ceiling is generous; test lacks explicit lower-bound assertion. Imposing new conditions beyond the original review = scope creep.
votes.what_would_change_mind: Evidence subprocess.run is intercepted by fixture; CI run >= ceiling; _CLI_ARGV patching bypasses subprocess.run path.

votes.top_risk: _cli_available is patched to always return True, bypassing availability-check code path; if council.py has early-return branches before reaching subprocess.run those remain untested.
votes.what_would_change_mind: Evidence conftest.py patches subprocess.run globally; evidence _CLI_ARGV redirect routes through independent internal timeout rather than subprocess.run(timeout=...); third case showing _cli_available bypass masks a production branch.

high_signal_3_plus_models: Wall-clock ceiling (3.0s) is generous — test lacks a tight lower-bound assertion to prove parallelism is mandatory rather than coincidental
CI timing jitter on resource-constrained runners could cause occasional flakiness
notable_1_to_2_models: Copilot: _cli_available patch bypasses availability-check code paths — dispatch paths before subprocess.run are untested
Codex: 1.01s observation is one bounded scenario, not a stress test across scheduler/load variations

pi_c2_advisory.disposition: Advisory — 4 inline docstrings in daemon/council.py address the ADR mutation carve-out. Proportionate. All 6 models confirm not a blocker.

hitl_judge.decision: HITL PAUSE LIFTED — full consensus achieved, blocking condition resolved

verdict: APPROVE — 6-0 unanimous. The single convergent condition (real-subprocess timeout test) is conclusively met by commit 80bfb3a. No remaining blockers.
